other_observers: Lisa Williams, Steamboat Springs.; Nancy Merrill, of Hayden may have been the first to identify it as a varied thrush, Andrew Spenser identified it as a ad. female thrush on 02/02/2008.
specific_location: 751 Princeton Ave. Steamboat Springs. Serafy residence. 970-879-5424. This residence in the "old town" section of the city.
description: This robin sized bird was obviously different from normal feeder birds found in Steamboat Springs in the winter. Observations were at 20-40 feet, in good light with and without binoculars.  The yellow-orange throat, grey chest band, and pink legs were the first features observed. The back was grey with some streaking, breast dull orange to white near legs, head grey with prominent dull white eye stripe, dark long (thrush like) bill. There appeared to be 1 1/2 orange tinted wing bars.Behaviors: Posture was much like a robin when perched, fed by picking through seeds available at feeder station, and flew with wing beat similar to a robin.Call: nonePlumage: adult
